title: erythroxylum undulatum
intro: Erythroxylum undulatum, also known as the "Peruvian coca", is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves have been used to treat various health conditions, such as altitude sickness, fatigue, and digestive issues, providing relief and promoting overall well-being. The leaves are typically consumed as a tea or used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and tinctures, which can help to alleviate symptoms and support the body's natural healing processes.
